title: Case study — bc-subscriptions
summary: BC-native subscription platform — methodology recursively applied. 38+ ADRs, 5-persona journey template, mechanical state derivation, runnable prototype as the design oracle, shipping code under apps/.

strategy.decision: Lead the commerce-bc cluster with bc-subscriptions — it's the methodology recursion play (big-blueprint applied to itself to drive its own ADR discipline). Five compositions following the standard template: hero + schematic (dual-track agile: spec ↔ prototype ↔ code) + approach (cite ADR-0001 Hive workflow + ADR-0029 marketplace-first reversal + ADR-0030 convention catalog) + quotable (one ADR pull) + outcome (38 ADRs, state-derive, prototype-as-oracle).
strategy.why: ADR-0009 cluster IA lists bc-subscriptions as the commerce-bc lead. It's the load-bearing example of 'methodology produces shipping code' — directly proves the production-line claim by counter-example (this is what happens when the methodology is applied recursively to itself). Repo is private but the artifacts (ADRs, methodology docs) are citable.
strategy.shipped: v2 /work/bc-subscriptions — case page with prose + GitHub link (private repo, so no clickable preview).
strategy.gap: Proposed = nested route + dual-track schematic + ADR quote pulls; Shipped = prose + private GitHub link.
strategy.question: How do we cite specific ADRs (ADR-0001, ADR-0029, ADR-0030, ADR-0037, ADR-0038) when the repo is private? Reviewer can't click through to verify. Decide: paraphrase + GitHub-permalink (auth-gated), or pull excerpts directly into the page copy.

currentState.summary: v2 case page with prose + private GitHub link (no clickable preview).
currentState.annotation: Route changes from flat to nested. private badge made explicit. Schematic introduced. Decide ADR-citation policy.
